prompt:
Use case: identity-preserve
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: remake the Myrmidons Hunter so it preserves the original hunter concept but visually belongs to the approved Tide Legionary and Spectre squad family
Subject: the same amphibious dual-pistol gunfighter with crest silhouette, lean athletic build, scavenged harness, and fast ranged-combat posture, but rendered with the chunkier readable shape language and cleaner Myrmidon anatomy established by the approved Tide Legionary and Spectre
Style/medium: moody post-apocalyptic tactics sprite illustration with painterly pixel-art-inspired rendering, stronger pixel clustering, larger readable forms, and reduced micro-detail for downscaling
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, facing slightly left, grounded battlefield-ready stance, transparent background, tight sprite bounds, readable when rendered around 100 to 110 px tall in game
Lighting/mood: restrained cold bioluminescent gloom with controlled contrast, practical faction lighting, no heavy bloom
Color palette: sea green skin, kelp black, brine cyan, rust leather, muted red-orange crest, restrained biotech teal accents
Constraints: preserve the recognizable hunter identity from the concept, preserve the dual-pistol silhouette, keep the body fully opaque and solid with alpha only outside the outer silhouette, make the character read as lighter and faster than Tide Legionary but more grounded and physically coherent than Spectre, match the approved Myrmidon unit family language from the two reference sprites, exactly one subject, no floor, no scenery, no text, no extra subjects, no oversized empty padding
Avoid: ammo belt, bulky heavy armor, human soldier proportions, glossy sci-fi rendering, anime rendering, photorealism, extra weapons, transparent holes, halo haze, cinematic background, noisy micro-texture
